title: J. Wheatley to replace A. Newey as Aston Martin Team Principal
desc: Rumors are spreading around the paddock that Adrian Newey has pushed his former colleague up to Lawrence Stroll. Huge vibrations from the Honda Power Unit make the AMR26 undriveable, preventing Alonso and Stroll from seeing the chequered flag of the first two rounds.

title: DS Automobile to quit Formula E
desc: The French automotive brand will not take part in the 2026-27 season. DS Automobiles is one of the most decorated manufacturers in Formula E history, famously becoming the first brand to secure back-to-back double titles. Since joining the series in Season 2, the marque has secured four titles, 18 victories, and 55 podiums across more than 139 races.

title: WRC to race in UK from 2027
desc: Developed in partnership with Motorsport UK, the new gravel-based event will be based in north-east Scotland. Stages will run  across the surrounding regions of Aberdeenshire and Moray.

title: Moto GP to race in Adelaide from 2027
desc: MotoGP has confirmed that the Australian Grand Prix will move to the Adelaide Street Circuit from 2027, marking a first of its kind event for the sport

title: Kick-off race cancelled for safety reasons
desc: The season opener of the ADAC RAVENOL Nurburgring endurance series has been cancelled. Low temperatures and snowfall had initially delayed the start to time training. The track conditions improved, but not the temperatures.

title: Qatar GP Postponed to November
desc: MotoGP organisers have rescheduled the Qatar GP to November 8th due to regional conflicts, resulting in a late-season calendar reshuffle for Portugal and Valencia.

title: Bahrain & Saudi GPs Cancelled
desc: Formula 1 is set to cancel the upcoming races in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia due to ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, reducing the 2026 calendar to 22 races.

title: Alpine to Exit Hypercar
desc: Alpine boss Philippe Sinault has confirmed the French manufacturer will end its factory Hypercar programme in the World Endurance Championship at the end of the 2026 season.

title: Qatar 1812 Postponed
desc: Due to the ongoing conflict in the middle east, the FIA has decided to postpone the race in Qatar to "the second half of the season" for now
